title: Simon Property Group: A-Rated Quality REIT At A Bargain Price
text: Simon Property Group has recovered from the pandemic, with improved occupancy rates, retailer sales per square foot, and average base rent for its U.S. malls and premium outlets. Despite challenges from e-commerce and a potential recession, SPG has managed to raise rents and maintain occupancy rates, indicating a healthy business environment for malls. SPG is currently undervalued, offering investors a wide margin of safety and the potential for double-digit returns in the coming years.

title: Better High Yield Buy: Simon Property Group Or Federal Realty?
text: FRT and SPG are two premier publicly traded retail REITs. SPG offers a higher yield at present and has a higher credit rating. However, FRT has a more defensive portfolio and has a much more impressive dividend growth history. We compare them and then offer our opinion on which is the better buy at the moment.

title: Billionaire Investor Says 'Sell Real Estate'
text: Billionaire Charlie Munger recently warned investors. He said that commercial real estate is facing severe challenges.

title: Simon Property: A 7.2% Yield For Cheap, I'm Jumping In
text: Simon Property Group last raised its quarterly dividend payout by 2.77% for a forward dividend yield that sits at 7.2%. The retail REIT is down by around 13% since the start of the year, with a price-to-forward FFO multiple of 8.49x. This is 28% lower than its peer group median.

title: Dividend Investing Like The Legendary Sam Zell
text: Sam Zell was known as the "Grave Dancer" for buying distressed but quality assets for pennies on the dollar and selling them later at the tippy top of the market. Zell was a master value investor, applying standard value investing principles to the world of commercial real estate.

title: Simon (SPG) Partners With The Webster to Revamp Luxury Shopping
text: Simon (SPG) collaborates with The Webster to introduce three new luxury boutiques. This strategic move promises to transform the luxury shopping landscape.

title: Why I Stopped Buying Treasuries To Buy REITs Instead
text: Treasuries now offer a 5.5% yield. Many investors are turning to Treasuries for safety.

title: Why Is Simon Property's Dividend So High?
text: The Federal Reserve and the banking crisis have put pressure on the REIT sector. Simon just upped its forecast and increased the dividend.

title: 3 Dividend Stocks Yielding 4%+ That Are Screaming Buys Right Now
text: Crown Castle has the market and the chops to spring back from a price drop. Brookfield Asset Management expects to raise its 4% dividend at a more than 15% annual pace.

title: Simon (SPG) Partners With Five Below to Boost Sales & Traffic
text: Simon (SPG) declares a partnership expansion with a leading retailer of trendy products for teens and tweens to open more stores at its properties by 2023.

title: REITs Will Go Bankrupt? Not So Fast
text: REITs are facing an "existential crisis" according to some investors. They claim that REITs will go bankrupt as they fail to refinance their debt.

title: Getting Ready For A 'Higher-For-Longer' Scenario For REITs
text: In the past decade, many REITs have become complacent and devised aggressive financing strategies that were underpinned by low interest rates. The sudden shift in the interest rates and a probability of a "higher-for-longer" scenario can put a notable share of REITs in a "nuclear winter" territory in my view.

title: Should You Buy the 5 Highest-Paying Dividend Stocks In the S&P 500?
text: There are red flags with each of the five highest-yielding dividend stocks on the S&P 500. A better option is an ETF that screens for the top dividend yields and lowest payout ratios.

title: 2 New Buy Alerts With A 6% Dividend Yield And High Potential Upside
text: REITs are today priced at historically high dividend yields. Even blue-chips now yield 6%.

title: Simon Property Group: Opportunity Knocks
text: Simon Property Group has proven to be a quality play in the retail space, with solid and growing operating metrics. It maintains plenty of liquidity and pays a well-covered and growing dividend.

title: 3 Top REITs To Buy In May
text: REITs as a sector have been hit extremely hard during this rate hike environment, but we are nearing the end of this cycle. Higher rates have created deep value opportunities in high-quality REITs.

title: 2 Investment-Grade Stocks With Big Dividends: Diamondback And Simon Property
text: We look at two bargain stocks with low price-earnings (P/E) ratios and big growth prospects. Pick #1: Diamondback (FANG) is an investment-grade company and carries a forward P/E ratio of just 6 times despite high growth.

title: Simon Property Group: Common Stock Heads South
text: The Simon Property Group, Inc. common stock price is going down. The dividend was increased yet again.

title: Realty Income And Simon Property Group: Graham Numbers Say It's REIT Time
text: The REIT sector is currently the second most attractive market sector in terms of its yield spread against risk-free interest rates. After a closer examination, we concluded that the sector's attractiveness is not caused by a few dominant stocks.

text: In real estate, different asset classes offer various attributes tailored to investor needs. For example, some real estate portfolios focus primarily on appreciating the underlying assets.

title: Simon Property Group's Strong Start to 2023 Suggests Malls Are Here to Stay
text: Simon Property Group (SPG), the largest shopping center manager and developer in the United States, has had a favorable start to the year, as indicated in its first-quarter report on Tuesday, which presented gains across different metrics.

title: Simon Property (SPG) Misses on Q1 FFO, Raises '23 Outlook

title: Simon Property (SPG) Q1 FFO Miss Estimates
text: Simon Property (SPG) came out with quarterly funds from operations (FFO) of $2.74 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.80 per share. This compares to FFO of $2.78 per share a year ago.
